frame_analyses.description: Pre-tied ligating loop visible in hand, held outside trainer box. Loop material and pusher clearly present.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop properly loaded and ready for introduction.

frame_analyses.description: Loop being manipulated in hands outside trainer. Two hands controlling loop and pusher assembly.
Circled view shows loop being positioned near foam appendage (red target visible in circled area). Loop appears open and ready to be placed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop is opened and approaching target appendage in trainer field.

frame_analyses.description: Circled magnified view shows loop in closer proximity to appendage. Loop opening is visible and appears to be centering on the marked appendage.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Careful positioning of loop over target site.

frame_analyses.description: Circled view shows loop fully positioned around appendage base. Loop encircles the appendage at what appears to be the marked site.
Hands visible manipulating pusher rod outside trainer. Performer is breaking pusher at the scored mark line visible on the plastic rod.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Breaking pusher at the correct scored location.

frame_analyses.description: Pusher rod partially separated, showing the break point. Pusher segment being held and prepared for advancement into trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pusher break completed successfully; rod now segmented.

frame_analyses.description: Hands manipulating separated pusher rod, advancing it through the trainer port to cinch loop on appendage.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady advancement of pusher to secure knot.

frame_analyses.description: Pusher rod being carefully advanced further. Hands show controlled force application.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled cinching motion with appropriate tension.

frame_analyses.description: Pusher rod continues advancement. Loop is being cinched tightly around appendage base at marked location.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop appears to be tightening progressively.

frame_analyses.description: Loop tail visible extending from trainer. Performer is holding tail and preparing to cut it with scissors or cutting instrument.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Tail accessible and ready for cutting.

frame_analyses.description: Loop tail being cut. Scissors or cutting instrument engaged with tail material inside or at margin of trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cut of loop tail material.

penalties.description: Pusher advancement and cinching phase took 46 seconds, significantly longer than expert benchmark (~15-20s). This suggests either excessive force application time, difficulty achieving knot security, or cautious/overly slow advancement.

loop_placement.notes: Loop appears to be positioned at or very near the marked appendage base in circled magnified views. Exact millimeter deviation cannot be determined from angle and zoom level.

loop_security.notes: No visual evidence of loop slipping after cinching completed. However, final state of knot security is partially obscured by hand position in later frames.

pusher_break.notes: Break point clearly visible on pusher rod in frame 7; break occurred at the correct scored location.

tail_cut.notes: Tail appears to be cut inside or at the port margin of the trainer. Final tail state visible in frame 12.

foam_appendage_assessment.notes: Appendage appears intact in circled views. Cannot fully assess compression/damage due to limited visibility of appendage detail after loop cinching.

confidence_rationale: Completion time and phase structure are well-observed from clear hand manipulations and visible loop/pusher components. Primary uncertainty: (1) exact mm deviation of final knot placement from mark—circled magnified views suggest good alignment but oblique angle prevents precise measurement; (2) final knot security state—loop does not appear to slip, but hands obscure appendage in final frames; (3) extent of appendage compression/tissue assessment limited by circled view constraints. Extended pusher phase duration is clearly documented and suggests either learning curve or technical difficulty.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation of final knot position from mark—camera zoom and angle make precise measurement impossible
Final appendage compression assessment—foam detail obscured by performer's hands in magnified circled views during cinching
Whether loop was pre-loaded or pre-broken before timing began—first frame shows loop already assembled outside trainer
technique_summary: Task completed successfully with proper loop placement, correct pusher breaking, and confirmed tail cut. Performance is competent but slow, particularly in the pusher advancement/cinching phase (46s vs. expert 15-20s). Loop placement appears accurate and knot does not visibly slip. No evidence of appendage damage or transection. Overall proficiency: intermediate to novice.
strengths: Successful task completion with secured loop at apparent mark location
Proper pusher break at scored line
No observable loop slippage or appendage damage
improvement_suggestions: Reduce pusher advancement/cinching time from 46s to under 20s through practice and confidence-building
Increase advancement speed while maintaining control—extended cinching time may indicate hesitation or fear of over-tightening
Practice loop placement to ensure consistent centering on marked appendage base
